title: CSS Selectors, Tree Navigation, Cardinality, Text, and Attributes · Runtime Lens: Selector Cardinality
summary: A brittle absolute selector returns the wrong repeated card, missing attributes become strings, nested text collapses, and a parser upgrade silently changes cardinality. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the selector cardinality increment in selector and extraction contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory…
objectives: State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
Use type, class, ID, attribute, combinator, grouping, and supported pseudo-class selectors with explicit scope and library support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-selector-cardinality — State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-selectors-tree-extraction-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-selectors-tree-extraction-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_selectors_tree_extraction_2(component_nodes: int = 8, extracted_records: int = 8, ambiguous_matches: int = 0, missing_fields: int = 3, provenance_nodes: int = 8) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if component_nodes < 1 or extracted_records < 0:
        return False, extracted_records, "selector-count"
    if extracted_records != component_nodes:
        return False, extracted_records, "record-cardinality"
    if ambiguous_matches > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_matches, "ambiguous-selector"
    if missing_fields < 0 or provenance_nodes != extracted_records:
        return False, provenance_nodes, "field-provenance"
    assert extracted_records == component_nodes and ambiguous_matches == 0
    return True, extracted_records, "provenance-selector-contract"

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory site-quality team handles case 3543 during a clean package and recovery drill. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile selector scope, cardinality, relative fields, text policy, missingness, and node provenance; no block, CAPTCHA, identity, or fingerprint evasion is permitted; then change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Taking the first match is harmless when a selector returns too many nodes.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches. Artifact under review: selector and extraction contract.

options.text: For inspect selector cardinality evidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Selector Cardinality for selector and extraction contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Selector Cardinality, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Selector Cardinality record says selector and extraction contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect selector cardinality evidence boundary for Selector Cardinality.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: State zero, one, optional, at-least-one, and many cardinality expectations and reject ambiguous or duplicate matches.
